engineIntelligence.h2: Rolls Royce Phantom VII Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the N73B68 6.75L V12 engine fitted to the Rolls Royce Phantom VII in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Rolls Royce Phantom VII N73B68 engine - petrol 6.75L replacement UK
knownFailures: Double VANOS failure - variable valve timing solenoids wear before 150,000 km causing rattling and power loss.
Timing chain stretch - chain tensioners weaken after 150,000 km leading to cold-start rattles.
Valvetronic actuator issues - low-speed vibrations and rough idle from motor or sensor faults.

cards.altText: Rolls Royce Phantom VII N73B68A engine - petrol 6.75L replacement UK
knownFailures: Direct injection coking - carbon build-up on intake valves causes misfires and reduced performance.
Oil leak from valve cover gaskets - high-temperature operation accelerates gasket degradation.

cards.altText: Rolls Royce Phantom VII N73B68C engine - petrol 6.75L replacement UK
knownFailures: High-pressure fuel pump wear - direct injection system components degrade causing hard starts.
Rear main seal oil leak - crankshaft seal failure leads to oil loss and potential engine damage.

engineGuide.h2: Rolls Royce Phantom VII Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Rolls Royce Phantom VII across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 6.75L Petrol
entries.history: BMW-sourced naturally aspirated V12 powering all Phantom VII variants, renowned for smoothness but requiring specialist maintenance.
compatibleVariants: Phantom, Phantom EWB, Phantom Drophead Coupe, Phantom Coupe
commonFailures: Double VANOS failure - variable valve timing solenoids wear before 150,000 km causing rattling and power loss.
Timing chain stretch - chain tensioners weaken after 150,000 km leading to cold-start rattles.
Valvetronic actuator issues - low-speed vibrations and rough idle from motor or sensor faults.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N73B68 Rolls Royce Phantom VII engine replacement

entries.title: 6.75L Petrol
entries.history: Early production N73 variant with Bosch Motronic engine management, fitted to 2003-2008 Phantom VII models.
commonFailures: Direct injection coking - carbon build-up on intake valves causes misfires and reduced performance.
Oil leak from valve cover gaskets - high-temperature operation accelerates gasket degradation.
entries.cta: Get quotes for N73B68A Rolls Royce Phantom VII engine replacement

entries.title: 6.75L Petrol
entries.history: Mid-production N73 variant with updated ECU mapping, fitted to facelift 2009-2012 Phantom VII models.
compatibleVariants: Phantom (2009-2012), Phantom EWB (2009-2012), Phantom Drophead Coupe (2009-2012)
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump wear - direct injection system components degrade causing hard starts.
Valvetronic low-speed vibration - actuator motor or position sensor faults at idle.

entries.title: 6.75L Petrol
entries.history: Final production N73 variant with Valvetronic updates, fitted to 2012-2017 Phantom VII models.
commonFailures: Rear main seal oil leak - crankshaft seal failure leads to oil loss and potential engine damage.
Timing chain guide wear - plastic guides degrade causing chain noise and potential failure.

commonProblems.h2: Rolls Royce Phantom VII Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Rolls Royce Phantom VII's N73B68 6.75L V12 is a sophisticated engine requiring specialist care. Double VANOS failure, timing chain stretch and Valvetronic issues dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Double VANOS Failure - Why N73B68 Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Phantom VII Phantom, Phantom EWB, Drophead Coupe, Coupe (2003-2017, N73B68/A/B/C)
problems.rootCause: Variable valve timing solenoids and actuators wear from oil contamination and heat cycling, causing camshaft timing errors.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty solenoids and seals, reset adaptation values
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early before actuator damage; 20,000-40,000 mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, rebuild or replace VANOS units, update software
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause; suitable for engines under 120,000 miles with good compression

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully reconditioned N73 with upgraded VANOS components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage engines or when bottom-end wear is present

problems.recommendation: On a Phantom VII valued at £35,000-£70,000, a £14,000-£20,000 rebuilt engine with warranty often represents better long-term value than repeated VANOS repairs, especially if the engine has exceeded 120,000 miles.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt N73B68 engine prices for your Rolls Royce Phantom VII

problems.h4: Timing Chain Stretch - Cold Start Rattles on N73 Engines
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Phantom VII all variants (2003-2017, N73B68/A/B/C)
problems.rootCause: Chain tensioners lose pressure and plastic guides wear, allowing the timing chain to stretch and create noise on cold starts.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace hydraulic tensioners and guide rails, reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if chain stretch is minimal; requires precise diagnosis

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully reconditioned N73 with upgraded timing components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended when chain stretch exceeds specifications or valve timing is compromised

problems.recommendation: Timing chain issues on the N73 are rarely economical to repair in isolation. Given the labour intensity and risk of collateral damage, a rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components typically offers better value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get quotes for N73 timing chain replacement Rolls Royce Phantom VII

problems.h4: Valvetronic Actuator Failure - Low-Speed Vibration Issues
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Phantom VII all variants (2003-2017, N73B68/A/B/C)
problems.rootCause: Valvetronic motor, eccentric shaft sensor or actuator gears wear, causing irregular valve lift and rough idle at low speeds.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty sensor or motor, recalibrate Valvetronic system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if fault codes point to electrical components only

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace eccentric shaft and associated components
repairOptions.longevity: Complex repair; only justified on low-mileage engines with otherwise excellent condition

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully reconditioned N73 with upgraded Valvetronic components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution for high-mileage engines or when multiple Valvetronic faults are present

problems.recommendation: Valvetronic repairs are highly labour-intensive. For Phantom VII owners facing multiple Valvetronic fault codes, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components often proves more cost-effective than piecemeal repairs.
problems.cta: Compare Valvetronic repair vs replacement costs for your Rolls Royce Phantom VII

problems.h4: Direct Injection Coking - Carbon Build-Up on Intake Valves
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Phantom VII all variants (2003-2017, N73B68/A/B/C)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection prevents fuel from washing intake valves, allowing carbon deposits to accumulate and restrict airflow.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake manifold, blast valves with walnut media to remove carbon
repairOptions.longevity: Effective preventative maintenance; recommended every 50,000 miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully reconditioned N73 with cleaned or replaced components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only necessary if coking has caused severe valve damage or compression loss

problems.recommendation: Regular walnut blasting every 50,000 miles is the most cost-effective approach to managing direct injection coking on the N73. Engine replacement is only warranted if carbon damage has compromised valve sealing.
problems.cta: Enquire about walnut blasting services for your Rolls Royce Phantom VII N73

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Rolls Royce Phantom VII
finalCta.paragraph: The N73B68 is a complex V12 requiring specialist knowledge. Whether you're facing VANOS failure, timing chain stretch or Valvetronic issues, EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who understand Phantom VII engines. Compare quotes for rebuilt, reconditioned or used N73 units - all backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and available for supply & fit nationwide.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Rolls Royce Phantom VII Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Rolls Royce Phantom VII and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Rolls Royce Phantom VII Petrol Engines
items.description: The Rolls Royce Phantom VII was exclusively powered by the BMW-sourced N73B68 6.75L naturally aspirated petrol V12 across all variants and production years.
items.descriptor: The Rolls Royce Phantom VII was exclusively powered by the BMW-sourced N73B68 6.75L naturally aspirated petrol V12 across all variants and production years.
items.familiesLabel: Common Rolls Royce Phantom VII Petrol Engine Families
families: N73B68 - base engine code for all Phantom VII variants, 460bhp naturally aspirated V12
N73B68A - early production variant (2003-2008) with Bosch Motronic engine management
N73B68B - mid-production facelift variant (2009-2012) with updated ECU mapping
N73B68C - final production variant (2012-2017) with Valvetronic refinements
knownFor: Exceptionally smooth and refined power delivery characteristic of Rolls-Royce
Double VANOS variable valve timing failures common before 150,000 km
Timing chain stretch requiring attention after 150,000 km
High specialist labour costs due to complex Valvetronic and direct injection systems

engineYears.h2: Rolls Royce Phantom VII Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Rolls Royce Phantom VII production timeline (2003-2017), organised so owners can quickly identify their N73B68 engine variant and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Phantom VII launched in 2003 with the new BMW-sourced N73B68A 6.75L V12, replacing the previous generation's naturally aspirated V12 architecture.
keyChanges: Phantom VII (RR1) launched with all-new N73B68A engine
Introduction of Valvetronic, Double VANOS and direct injection technology
Phantom Extended Wheelbase (EWB) variant introduced in 2005
knownFor: Exceptional refinement and effortless torque delivery
Early N73B68A units prone to direct injection coking on intake valves
Valve cover gasket oil leaks common due to high operating temperatures
Complex electronics require Rolls-Royce specialist diagnostic equipment
years.cta: Get quotes for 2003-2008 Rolls Royce Phantom VII replacement engines
years.ticker: Rolls Royce Phantom VII N73B68A engine replacement • Phantom VII direct injection coking repair • N73B68A valve cover gasket leak
enquiries: Rolls Royce Phantom VII N73B68A engine replacement
Phantom VII direct injection coking repair

years.description: The 2009 facelift introduced the updated N73B68B engine with refined ECU mapping and improved Valvetronic calibration across new Drophead Coupe and Coupe variants.
keyChanges: N73B68B engine introduced with updated ECU software
Phantom Drophead Coupe (2007) and Phantom Coupe (2008) added to range
Minor revisions to Valvetronic actuator design for improved reliability
mainEngines: N73B68B - 6.75L petrol (Phantom, Phantom EWB, Drophead Coupe, Coupe)
knownFor: Improved low-speed refinement from Valvetronic calibration updates
Double VANOS solenoid failures emerging on higher-mileage examples
Timing chain tensioner wear becoming apparent on 100,000+ mile units
Specialist labour costs remain high due to engine complexity
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2012 Rolls Royce Phantom VII replacement engines
years.ticker: Rolls Royce Phantom VII N73B68B engine replacement • Phantom VII Double VANOS failure • N73B68B timing chain tensioner replacement
enquiries: Rolls Royce Phantom VII N73B68B engine replacement

years.preview: Phantom VII Final Production & N73B68C Refinements
years.description: Final production years featured the N73B68C engine with further Valvetronic refinements and updated ancillary components before Phantom VIII introduction in 2017.
keyChanges: N73B68C engine introduced with final Valvetronic and ancillary updates
Production of Phantom Coupe ended in 2016, Drophead Coupe in 2016
Final Phantom saloon and EWB units built in 2017 before Phantom VIII launch
knownFor: Most refined iteration of the N73 architecture with reduced failure rates
Rear main seal oil leaks still possible on high-mileage examples
Direct injection coking remains a maintenance consideration regardless of variant
Strong residual values support investment in quality rebuilt engines
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2017 Rolls Royce Phantom VII replacement engines
years.ticker: Rolls Royce Phantom VII N73B68C engine replacement • Phantom VII rear main seal replacement • N73B68C direct injection maintenance
enquiries: Rolls Royce Phantom VII N73B68C engine replacement

faq.h2: Rolls Royce Phantom VII Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Rolls Royce Phantom VII engine?
items.answer: A rebuilt N73B68 engine for the Phantom VII typically costs £4,200-£5,800 supply only, or £14,000-£20,000 fully rebuilt with warranty. Supply & fit services range from £12,995-£18,000 including specialist labour.
keyPoints: Supply-only rebuilt N73B68 engines start from £4,200
Fully rebuilt units with upgraded components cost £14,000-£20,000
Supply & fit service adds £8,000-£12,000 for specialist labour
All EngineMarket suppliers include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare rebuilt N73B68 engine prices for your Rolls Royce Phantom VII

items.question: What is the engine code for the Rolls Royce Phantom VII?
items.answer: All Rolls Royce Phantom VII variants (2003-2017) use the BMW-sourced N73B68 6.75L V12 petrol engine, with suffix variants N73B68A, N73B68B and N73B68C denoting production updates.
keyPoints: Base engine code: N73B68 6.75L naturally aspirated V12
N73B68A: Early production (2003-2008) with Bosch Motronic
N73B68B: Facelift models (2009-2012) with updated ECU
N73B68C: Final production (2012-2017) with Valvetronic refinements
items.cta: Get quotes for your specific N73B68 variant Rolls Royce Phantom VII

items.question: What are the most common Rolls Royce Phantom VII engine problems?
items.answer: The N73B68 engine commonly experiences Double VANOS failure before 150,000 km, timing chain stretch after 150,000 km, and Valvetronic actuator issues causing low-speed vibrations.
keyPoints: Double VANOS solenoid/actuator wear causes rattling and power loss
Timing chain stretch leads to cold-start rattles and potential valve damage
Valvetronic motor or sensor faults create rough idle and vibration
Direct injection coking requires walnut blasting every 50,000 miles
items.cta: Compare repair vs replacement costs for your Rolls Royce Phantom VII N73

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Rolls Royce Phantom VII engine?
items.answer: Given Phantom VII values of £35,000-£70,000, a £14,000-£20,000 rebuilt N73B68 engine with warranty is often economically viable compared to vehicle depreciation or scrappage.
keyPoints: Phantom VII residual values support investment in quality rebuilt engines
Rebuilt N73 with warranty offers better long-term value than repeated repairs
Supply & fit service simplifies the process with single-point responsibility
EngineMarket specialists understand Phantom VII complexity and warranty requirements
items.cta: Find out if engine replacement makes sense for your Rolls Royce Phantom VII

items.question: Can I fit a BMW N73 engine from a 760Li into my Phantom VII?
items.answer: Yes, the N73B68 long block is shared with the BMW E66 760Li, but Phantom-specific ancillaries, wiring and ECU coding require specialist adaptation for successful installation.
keyPoints: N73B68 cylinder block, heads and internals are cross-compatible
Phantom-specific ancillaries (alternator, AC compressor) differ from BMW
ECU coding and immobiliser integration require Rolls-Royce specialist tools
EngineMarket suppliers verify compatibility before dispatch to avoid fitment issues
items.cta: Enquire about N73B68 cross-compatibility for your Rolls Royce Phantom VII

items.question: How long does a Rolls Royce Phantom VII N73 engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, N73B68 engines can achieve 200,000+ miles. Key service items include timing chain inspection at 150,000 km and regular walnut blasting for direct injection coking.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes with 5W-40 fully synthetic are critical for longevity
Timing chain and VANOS components require attention around 150,000 km
Direct injection coking management via walnut blasting every 50,000 miles
Specialist servicing extends life beyond typical high-mileage failure points
items.cta: Get maintenance advice for your Rolls Royce Phantom VII N73 engine

items.question: What oil does a Rolls Royce Phantom VII N73 engine use?
items.answer: The N73B68 requires 5W-40 fully synthetic oil meeting BMW Longlife-01 specification, with a capacity of 8.5 litres including filter change.
keyPoints: Use 5W-40 fully synthetic oil meeting BMW Longlife-01 spec
Total capacity: 8.5 litres including filter replacement
Oil change interval: 10,000 miles or 12 months whichever comes first
Regular oil analysis recommended for high-mileage N73 engines
items.cta: Find oil change specialists for your Rolls Royce Phantom VII N73

items.question: Do you offer warranty on rebuilt Rolls Royce Phantom VII engines?
items.answer: Yes. All reconditioned and rebuilt N73B68 engines supplied through EngineMarket include a comprehensive 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty on all rebuilt units
Warranty covers parts and labour at any UK specialist workshop
Compression test results and build documentation provided with each engine
Extended warranty options available through select suppliers
items.cta: Compare warranty terms for rebuilt Rolls Royce Phantom VII engines

items.question: How long does Rolls Royce Phantom VII engine replacement take?
items.answer: Typical N73B68 replacement takes 5-7 working days due to V12 complexity, specialist tooling requirements and ECU coding procedures at Rolls-Royce trained workshops.
keyPoints: Engine removal and installation requires specialist lifting equipment
ECU coding and adaptation reset essential for proper operation
Road testing and final quality checks add 1-2 days to timeline
Supply & fit bookings should be scheduled in advance with specialists
items.cta: Book your Rolls Royce Phantom VII engine fitting slot today

items.question: Do you buy broken Rolls Royce Phantom VII engines?
items.answer: Yes. EngineMarket suppliers offer cash for broken N73B68 engines as part of core exchange programmes, reducing your net replacement cost when upgrading to a rebuilt unit.
keyPoints: Core exchange credit typically £500-£1,500 depending on engine condition
Broken engines must be complete with no major external damage
Core collection arranged nationwide to simplify the exchange process
Core value applied directly to your rebuilt engine invoice
items.cta: Get a quote for your broken Rolls Royce Phantom VII N73 engine
